title: HitlPolicy
description: Spec §6a HITL Policy Primitive — structured object with 9 trigger types + 3 UI variants + notifier plugin interface. Locked at WI-16 (2026-04-18). v0.1 STANDARD-mode-hardcoded (per CLAUDE.md §3); mode-keyed HITL overrides in framework JSON are loaded but only STANDARD is evaluated at runtime.

HitlTrigger.title: HitlTrigger
HitlTrigger.description: Spec §6a HITL trigger types (9 values, locked). Each names a condition under which the runtime suspends to await human input. Exhaustive matching is required throughout the SDK; Rust enum exhaustiveness + this schema's `enum` closure are the two enforcement layers.

HitlUiVariant.title: HitlUiVariant
HitlUiVariant.description: Spec §6a UI variants. Panel: full takeover, dims graph, ARIA non-modal (graph queryable). Modal: floating dialog, blocks adjacent interaction, ARIA modal, Escape-closeable. Toast: non-blocking, auto-dismiss after 30s (treated as `notifier_failed` if no user response).

HitlNotifierType.title: HitlNotifierType
HitlNotifierType.description: Notifier plugin discriminator. The 3 built-in types ship in v1 (terminal_bell, desktop, sound). `plugin` is the open extension point — external notifier plugins from `notifiers/` dir under §8.security model; M9 generators wire the loader, M04.E ships only the 3 built-ins.

HitlNotifier.title: HitlNotifier
HitlNotifier.description: Notifier plugin shape per spec §6a. Built-in types ignore the `name` + `config` fields; plugin notifiers (type = `plugin`) require both. v0.1 plugin loader returns NotImplemented per M9 deferral.

enabled.description: Disabled notifiers are loaded but skipped at dispatch time.

name.description: Plugin identifier (required when `type = plugin`). Ignored for built-in types.

config.description: Plugin-specific configuration (required when `type = plugin`). Secrets reference via `secret://name` per spec §15d. Ignored for built-in types.

HitlTriggerPolicy.title: HitlTriggerPolicy
HitlTriggerPolicy.description: Per-trigger configuration. `enabled` is the master switch; `ui` overrides the default UI variant for this trigger; remaining fields are trigger-specific (e.g. `tools` for on_risky_tool, `threshold` for on_failure_threshold, `percent` for on_budget_threshold).

tools.description: Risky-tool match patterns for `on_risky_tool` (e.g. `Bash:rm`, `WebFetch:*`). Ignored for other triggers.

threshold.description: Failure count that fires `on_failure_threshold` (default 3 per spec §3a `max_failures`). Ignored for other triggers.

percent.description: Budget percent that fires `on_budget_threshold` (default 90). Ignored for other triggers.

title: HitlPolicy
description: Framework JSON `hitl_policy` field per spec §6a. Each of the 9 triggers is independently configurable; absent keys default to disabled.

notifiers.description: Notifiers fire in parallel when any enabled trigger evaluates. v0.1 ships terminal_bell + desktop + sound; plugin notifiers deferred to M9.

timeout_seconds.description: Wall-clock seconds the seam waits before treating the request as timed out. v0.1 default 1h per spec §6a; per-trigger override deferred to v1.0.

default_action_on_timeout.description: Free-text action token emitted in `hitl_timeout` events. SDK consumers route this to plan/task FSM transitions (e.g. `abort` → plan_aborted).
